description_long: This object consists of a group of cylindrical barrels arranged in two parallel rows, each barrel featuring a variety of bright colors such as red, yellow, blue, and orange. The barrels appear to be made of metal, with some showing signs of wear, rust, and faded paint, suggesting outdoor or industrial use. Each barrel has a closed top and bottom, with no visible openings or lids, and some display remnants of labels or markings, though these are mostly worn or partially obscured. The barrels are of uniform size and shape, with slightly rounded edges and a standard drum-like form. The surface texture is rough and matte, with visible dents and scratches indicating frequent handling or exposure to the elements. These barrels are commonly found in urban environments, such as construction sites, alleys, or storage yards, and are often used for storing or transporting liquids or materials.
description: A group of weathered, colorful metal barrels arranged in two rows, showing signs of rust, faded paint, and worn labels. The barrels are cylindrical, closed, and typical of those found in urban or industrial settings.
description_view_0: Two parallel rows of barrels viewed from the end, showing mostly the tops and a hint of their cylindrical sides.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full height and colorful sides of each barrel, with visible labels and surface wear.
description_view_2: Opposite end view similar to view 0, showing the tops of the barrels and their arrangement in two rows.
description_view_3: Side view from the opposite direction of view 1, again showing the full height and colorful, worn sides of the barrels.
